artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2020-12-31
artifacts.excerpt:
Hey folks, with the New Year almost upon us, here’s a reminder to ruthlessly cut out of your life anyone who is not adding at least $100,000 worth of value to you. 

If you don’t walk away from your experiences together thinking “I would’ve paid $100K for that,” get rid of them.

planets.name: Think
planets.summary: Archive cluster around think: 689 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2021-12-22
artifacts.excerpt:
Good copywriting is narrative therapy.

In other words:

“Knowing what your prospect’s story is, and how they think their current story is going to end… and then giving them a different ending.”

— @craigclemens & @Nicolascole77
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, something, question, lol.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2021-04-09
artifacts.excerpt:
I started to think back on my social life during all those years. During the years when I was working my first jobs. During college. During high school. Heck, even during middle school and elementary.

One thing stood out in common between all of them.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, something, question, lol.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2021-06-21
artifacts.excerpt:
Some people ignore nutrition and biochemistry in favor of emotional self-regulation through techniques like IFS. 

Others ignore emotional work in favor of nutrition.

For those of you who use both, how do you think about the purpose and benefits of each? https://t.co/kxcnnK5xEz
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, something, question, lol.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2022-04-19
artifacts.excerpt:
The more I use Twitter, the less I'm able to be alone with my thoughts. 

I think it's something to do with how this platform lets me instantly publish and share every little thing that I'm thinking. 

I get what I've been calling "Twitterbrain."
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, something, question, lol.

artifacts.title: Work receipt from 2020-12-07
artifacts.excerpt:
Anyone in this self-therapy corner of Twitter use Roam Research?

I've yet to give it a try but I'm particularly interested in using it for self-therapy purposes: journaling, keeping track of emotions, dreams, parts work, etc.

Curious if anyone's doing this already 😃

How would your living situation and day-to-day life evolve?
